name: NDPC General Application and Implementation Directive 2025
statute: General Application and Implementation Directive 2025 (effective 19 September 2025)

obligations.title: File DCMI/DPMI designation via NDPC Compliance Portal
obligations.description: Data Controllers and Processors of Major Importance must submit the GAID Form A registration via the NDPC Compliance Portal within six months of meeting the major-importance thresholds. Renew annually.

obligations.title: Engage an NDPC-licensed DPCO for annual compliance audit
obligations.description: DCMIs must appoint a Data Protection Compliance Organisation licensed by NDPC to conduct the annual compliance audit and prepare the Form C return.

obligations.title: File Annual Compliance Return (Form C) by 31 March
obligations.description: Submit the DPCO-attested annual compliance return to NDPC using GAID Form C no later than 31 March each year, covering the preceding calendar year.

obligations.title: Maintain cross-border transfer register citing GAID adequacy list
obligations.description: Record every transfer of personal data outside Nigeria, cite the adequacy basis (GAID Schedule 3 whitelist, SCCs, BCRs, or explicit derogation), and update the register within 30 days of any new transfer.

obligations.title: Implement parental consent verification for processing of children's data
obligations.description: Where services are likely to be accessed by children under 18, operate a verifiable parental-consent mechanism and document the method of verification.

obligations.title: Disclose automated decision-making and provide human-review option
obligations.description: Disclose in the privacy notice any solely-automated decision with legal or similarly significant effect, the logic involved, and the process for requesting human review.

obligations.title: Use GAID Schedule 2 template for breach notifications to NDPC
obligations.description: Breach notifications to NDPC must use the Schedule 2 template (nature, categories and approximate number of data subjects, likely consequences, measures taken) and be filed within 72 hours.

obligations.title: Execute GAID-aligned Data Processing Agreements with processors
obligations.description: Every engagement of a third-party processor must be governed by a written DPA containing the mandatory clauses listed in GAID Part VII, including sub-processor authorisation, audit rights, and return-or-destroy at termination.

obligations.title: Retain audit logs of personal-data processing for at least 7 years
obligations.description: Access, modification, and deletion logs relating to personal data must be retained for a minimum of seven years and produced to NDPC on request.

obligations.title: Deliver annual data-protection training to all staff handling personal data
obligations.description: Conduct role-appropriate data-protection training at least annually; keep attendance records and training materials for NDPC inspection.

obligations.title: Appoint a DPO with NDPC-recognised certification
obligations.description: The Data Protection Officer must hold a data-protection certification recognised by NDPC and the appointment must be notified via the Compliance Portal within 30 days of designation.

obligations.title: Pay annual DCMI/DPMI filing fee per GAID Schedule 1
obligations.description: Pay the tiered annual filing fee scaled to the organisation's classification under GAID Schedule 1 at the time of Form C filing.
